hacking.notes: Initial access via an internet-exposed REDCap server with inadequate access controls; UNC6508 likely exploited an older, vulnerable REDCap version (exact method undetermined per Google Threat Intelligence Group).

malware.name: INFINITERED (InfiniteRed)
malware.notes: Custom, previously undocumented malware deployed ~3 months after initial compromise; comprises a persistence module, a credential harvester, and a backdoor, hidden by trojanizing server system files. Actor also abused domain content-compliance rules (Google Workspace) as a novel exfiltration channel.

plus.analyst_notes:
Closes https://github.com/vz-risk/VCDB/issues/23388
Encoded by AI — encode-veris-incident skill version 20260618T195537Z

The PHIDBR2019 label was IGNORED per skill guidance (artifact/noise tag); the embedded '2019' is NOT the incident year — this compromise began September 2023.

SCOPE: This is part of a multiyear China-nexus espionage campaign (Google Threat Intelligence Group / Mandiant, actor UNC6508) that hit MULTIPLE independent US and Canadian medical/research organizations via self-hosted REDCap servers. These are independent victims of a campaign, NOT customers of a shared service provider, so the provider-breach rule does NOT apply and no secondary victims are listed. This record codes the single, unnamed 'North American medical institution' that the issue describes — the org GTIG details as compromised in September 2023 with activity persisting more than a year. A campaign_id could be assigned later if VCDB tracks the broader campaign.

ACTOR: UNC6508, a China-nexus state-sponsored espionage group (attributed by Google Threat Intelligence Group). variety='Nation-state', motive='Espionage' (acquiring biomedical/pharmaceutical IP and research). actor.country='CN' from the China-nexus attribution (the operators' physical location is not separately stated; coded from the documented PRC nexus).

ACTION: Hacking — 'Exploit vuln' via 'Web application' (internet-exposed, under-secured/legacy REDCap instance); the exact exploit is undetermined per GTIG, but vulnerable-version exploitation of an exposed web server is well-supported. Malware — INFINITERED ('InfiniteRed'): coded Backdoor + Trojan (trojanized server system files) + Capture stored data (credential harvester) + Export data (exfiltration); vector 'Direct install' (placed after the actor already had server access). The actor also abused Google Workspace domain content-compliance rules as a novel exfiltration channel (noted, not a distinct VERIS action variety). attribute.integrity 'Software installation' reflects the malware deployment / trojanized files.

ASSET: REDCap servers — S - Web application (the REDCap web platform) and S - Database (clinical research databases). cloud coded On-Premise + External Cloud: self-hosted REDCap servers (often on-prem/institutional) plus the abused Google Workspace cloud channel; specific hosting of the victim's instance not stated.

ATTRIBUTE: data_disclosure='Yes' — exfiltration of sensitive research data is confirmed by the reporting. data varieties: Medical (diagnostic data, treatment histories, clinical-trial/health data), Personal (study-participant identifiers), Secrets (biomedical/pharmaceutical research IP — the campaign's objective). state='Stored' (at rest in REDCap; encryption status not stated). data_total OMITTED — no record/participant count disclosed. data_abuse='Unknown' — espionage collection; no specific downstream misuse (fraud/identity theft/leak) reported.

TIMELINE: incident begin = September 2023 (GTIG: compromise of the medical research org began Sept 2023; INFINITERED deployed ~3 months later, ~Dec 2023). Malicious activity persisted >1 year (through ~November 2025); publicly reported June 2026. discovery coded ~2 years (long undetected dwell). dbir_year=2023.

VICTIM: Unnamed North American medical institution. industry coded '62' (the NAICS sector from the 'NAICS 62: Health Care and Social Assistance' label) — subsector not specified and the org is unnamed, so the 2-digit sector is used rather than guessing a subcode. country='Unknown' — the specific institution is described only as 'North American' (the campaign spans US and Canada); employee_count='Unknown' (org unnamed).

SOURCES: hipaapulse.com (issue link), bleepingcomputer.com, helpnetsecurity.com, securityweek.com, csoonline.com, cybersecuritydive.com, theregister.com, and Google Cloud Threat Intelligence blog (cloud.google.com).

event_chain.summary: Exploited a legacy/exposed REDCap web server vulnerability for initial access
INFINITERED deployed (~3 months later): backdoor + credential harvester via trojanized system files for persistence
Sensitive clinical-research data exfiltrated (incl. via abused Workspace content-compliance rules)

summary: A China-nexus state-sponsored espionage group tracked by Google Threat Intelligence Group as UNC6508 compromised an internet-exposed, under-secured REDCap clinical-research server at an unnamed North American medical institution, beginning in September 2023. About three months in, the actor deployed custom, previously undocumented malware (INFINITERED / 'InfiniteRed') — a backdoor, credential harvester, and persistence module hidden by trojanizing server system files — moved laterally, and exfiltrated sensitive research data (study-participant identifiers, diagnostic data and treatment histories, plus biomedical/pharmaceutical research IP), in part by abusing Google Workspace domain content-compliance rules. The intrusion went undetected for more than a year (through ~November 2025) and was disclosed publicly in June 2026 as part of a broader multiyear campaign against US and Canadian medical, military, and AI research organizations.

victim.victim_id: Unnamed North American medical research institution
